prompts.text: Vocabulary to introduce: 'high' and 'low' (pitch — position on the keyboard), 'loud' and 'quiet' (volume — how hard they press), 'fast' and 'slow' (tempo — speed of playing). Use all six words during free play.
Model musical exploration aloud: 'I wonder what it sounds like if I play only the high keys... now only the low ones.' Narrate your own exploration to show the vocabulary in use.
Questions to return to: 'Can you play something that sounds happy? Sad? Scary? Calm?' — connects music to emotion vocabulary.
Challenge: 'Can you play the same notes again and remember them?' — early pattern and musical memory work, no pressure.
